title: Listeria-based immunogenic compositions comprising heteroclitic wilms tumor protein antigens and methods of use thereof
abstract: Provided herein are recombinant fusion polypeptides comprising one or more antigenic Wilms tumor protein (WT1) peptides, wherein at least one of the antigenic WT1 peptides is a heteroclitic mutant WT1 peptide (e.g., fused to a PEST-containing peptide). Also provided are nucleic acids encoding such fusion polypeptides, recombinant bacteria or Listeria strains comprising such fusion polypeptides or such nucleic acids, and cell banks comprising such recombinant bacteria or Listeria strains. Also provided herein are methods of generating such fusion polypeptides, such nucleic acids, and such recombinant bacteria or Listeria strains. Also provided are immunogenic compositions, pharmaceutical compositions, and vaccines comprising such fusion polypeptides, such nucleic acids, or such recombinant bacteria or Listeria strains. Also provided are methods of inducing an anti-WT1 immune response in a subject, methods of inducing an anti-WT1-expressing-tumor or anti-WT1-expressing-cancer immune response in a subject, methods of treating a WT1-expressing or WT1-associated tumor or cancer in a subject, methods of preventing a WT1-expressing or WT1-associated tumor or cancer in a subject, and methods of protecting a subject against a WT1-expressing or WT1-associated tumor or cancer using such recombinant fusion polypeptides, nucleic acids, recombinant bacteria or Listeria strains, immunogenic compositions, pharmaceutical compositions, or vaccines.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. A recombinant Listeria strain comprising a nucleic acid comprising a first open reading frame encoding a fusion polypeptide, wherein the fusion polypeptide comprises a PEST-containing peptide fused to one or more antigenic Wilms tumor protein (WTl) peptides, wherein at least one of the one or more antigenic WTl peptides is a heteroclitic mutant WTl peptide. 2. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 1, wherein the heteroclitic mutant WTl peptide comprises the sequence set forth in any one of SEQ ID NOS: 98, 99, 103, 104, 106-126, and 152. 3. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 2, wherein the heteroclitic mutant WTl peptide comprises the sequence set forth in any one of SEQ ID NOS: 98, 99, 103, 104, 106, and 152. 4. A recombinant Listeria strain comprising a nucleic acid comprising a first open reading frame encoding a fusion polypeptide, wherein the fusion polypeptide comprises a PEST-containing peptide fused to one or more antigenic Wilms tumor protein (WTl) peptides, wherein the one or more antigenic WTl peptides comprise one or more of the sequences set forth in SEQ ID NOS: 98-106 and 152. 5. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the one or more antigenic WTl peptides comprise two or more antigenic WTl peptides. 6. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 5, wherein the two or more antigenic WTl peptides comprise a first peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 100, a second peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 101, a third peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 99, a fourth peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 103, a fifth peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 106, a sixth peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 152, and a seventh peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 98. 7. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 6, wherein the two or more antigenic WTl peptides are arranged in the fusion polypeptide in the following order: the first peptide, the second peptide, the third peptide, the fourth peptide, the fifth peptide, the sixth peptide, and the seventh peptide. 8. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 5, wherein the two or more antigenic WTl peptides comprise a first peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 100, a second peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 101, and a third peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 99. 9. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 8, wherein the combination of the two or more antigenic WTl peptides comprises the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 153. 10. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 9, wherein the fusion polypeptide comprises the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 150. 11. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 5, wherein the two or more antigenic WTl peptides further comprise a first peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 99, a second peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 105, a third peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 106, and a fourth peptide comprising the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 152. 12. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 11, wherein the combination of the two or more antigenic WTl peptides comprises the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 154. 13. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 12, wherein the fusion polypeptide comprises the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 151. 14. The recombinant Listeria strain of any one of claims 1-8 and 11, wherein the two or more antigenic WTl peptides are fused directly to each other without intervening sequence. 15. The recombinant Listeria strain of any one of claims 1-8 and 11, wherein the two or more antigenic WTl peptides are linked to each other via peptide linkers. 16. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 15, wherein one or more of the linkers set forth in SEQ ID NOS: 33-42 are used to link the two or more antigenic WTl peptides. 17. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the fusion polypeptide does not comprise a ubiquitin protein. 18. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the fusion polypeptide further comprises one or more peptide tags N-terminal and/or C-terminal to the one or more antigenic WT1 peptides. 19. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 18, wherein the one or more peptide tags comprise one or more of the following: 3xFLAG tag; 6xHis tag; SIINFEKL tag, and the FLAG tag set forth in SEQ ID NO: 155. 20. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the PEST-containing peptide is on the N-terminal end of the fusion polypeptide. 21. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the PEST-containing peptide is a listeriolysin O (LLO) protein or a fragment thereof or an ActA protein or a fragment thereof. 22. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 21, wherein the PEST- containing peptide is an N-terminal fragment of LLO. 23. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 22, wherein the N-terminal fragment of LLO has the sequence set forth in SEQ ID NO: 59. 24. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 21, wherein the PEST- containing peptide is the LLO protein or the fragment thereof and comprises a mutation in a cholesterol-binding domain. 25. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 24, wherein the LLO mutation comprises one of the following: (1) a substitution of residues C484, W491, or W492 of SEQ ID NO: 55 or corresponding substitutions when the LLO protein is optimally aligned with SEQ ID NO: 55 ; or (2) a deletion of 1-11 amino acids within the residues 483-493 of SEQ ID NO: 55 or a corresponding deletion when the LLO protein is optimally aligned with SEQ ID NO: 55. 26. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the nucleic acid is operably integrated into the Listeria genome. 27. The recombinant Listeria strain of any one of claims 1-25, wherein the nucleic acid is in an episomal plasmid. 28. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the nucleic acid does not confer antibiotic resistance upon the recombinant Listeria strain. 29. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the recombinant Listeria strain is attenuated. 30. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the recombinant Listeria strain is an auxotrophic Listeria strain. 31. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 29 or 30, wherein the attenuated Listeria strain comprises a mutation in one or more endogenous genes that inactivates the one or more endogenous genes. 32. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 31, wherein the one or more endogenous genes comprise prfA. 33. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 31, wherein the one or more endogenous genes comprise actA. 34. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 31, wherein the one or more endogenous genes comprise actA and MB. 35. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 31, wherein the one or more endogenous genes comprise actA, dal, and dat. 36. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the nucleic acid comprises a second open reading frame encoding a metabolic enzyme. 37. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 36, wherein the metabolic enzyme is an alanine racemase enzyme or a D-amino acid aminotransferase enzyme. 38. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the fusion polypeptide is expressed from an hly promoter, a prfA promoter, an actA promoter, or a p60 promoter. 39. The recombinant Listeria strain of claim 38, wherein the fusion 

 polypeptide is expressed from an hly promoter. 40. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the recombinant Listeria strain is a recombinant Listeria monocytogenes strain. 41. The recombinant Listeria strain of any one of claims 1-20, wherein the recombinant Listeria strain is an attenuated Listeria monocytogenes strain comprising a deletion of or inactivating mutation in prfA, wherein the nucleic acid is in an episomal plasmid and comprises a second open reading frame encoding a D133V PrfA mutant protein. 42. The recombinant Listeria strain of any one of claims 1-20, wherein the recombinant Listeria strain is an attenuated Listeria monocytogenes strain comprising a deletion of or inactivating mutation in actA, dal, and dat, wherein the nucleic acid is in an episomal plasmid and comprises a second open reading frame encoding an alanine racemase enzyme or a D-amino acid aminotransferase enzyme, and wherein the PEST-containing peptide is an N-terminal fragment of LLO. 43. The recombinant Listeria strain of any one of claims 1-20, wherein recombinant Listeria strain is an attenuated Listeria monocytogenes strain comprising a deletion of or inactivating mutation in actA and MB, wherein the nucleic acid is genomically integrated, and wherein the PEST-containing peptide is an ActA protein or a fragment thereof. 44. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the recombinant Listeria strain has been passaged through an animal host. 45. The recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim, wherein the recombinant Listeria strain is capable of escaping a phagosome. 46. An immunogenic composition comprising the recombinant Listeria strain of any preceding claim. 47. The immunogenic composition of claim 46, wherein the immunogenic comprises a combination of two or more recombinant Listeria strain, wherein each recombinant Listeria strain comprises a different set of antigenic WT1 peptides or the same set of antigenic WT1 peptides in a different order. 48. The immunogenic composition of claim 47, wherein each recombinant Listeria strain comprises a different set of antigenic WT1 peptides. 49. The immunogenic composition of any one of claims 46-48, wherein the immunogenic composition further comprises an adjuvant. 50. The immunogenic composition of claim 49, wherein the adjuvant comprises a detoxified listeriolysin O (dtLLO), a granulocyte/macrophage colony- stimulating factor (GM-CSF) protein, a nucleotide molecule encoding a GM-CSF protein, saponin QS21 , monophosphoryl lipid A, or an unmethylated CpG-containing oligonucleotide. 51. A method of inducing an immune response against a tumor or cancer in a subject, comprising administering to the subject the recombinant Listeria strain of any one of claims 1-45 or the immunogenic composition of any one of claims 46-50. 52. A method of preventing or treating a tumor or cancer in a subject, comprising administering to the subject the recombinant Listeria strain of any one of claims 1-45 or the immunogenic composition of any one of claims 46-50. 53. The method of claim 51 or 52, wherein multiple different recombinant Listeria strains or multiple immunogenic compositions are administered to the subject. 54. The method of claim 53, wherein the multiple different recombinant Listeria strains or multiple immunogenic compositions are administered to the subject simultaneously. 55. The method of claim 53, wherein the multiple different recombinant Listeria strains or multiple immunogenic compositions are administered to the subject sequentially. 56. The method of any one of claims 53-55, wherein the multiple different recombinant Listeria strains or multiple immunogenic compositions each comprises a different set of antigenic WT1 peptides or the same set of antigenic WT1 peptides in a different order. 57. The method of claim 56, wherein each recombinant Listeria strain or immunogenic composition comprises a different set of antigenic WT1 peptides. 58. The method of any one of claims 51-57, wherein the method further comprises administering an immune checkpoint inhibitor antagonist. 59. The method of claim 58, wherein the immune checkpoint inhibitor comprises an anti-PD- 1 antibody or an antigen-binding fragment thereof and/or an anti- CTLA-4 antibody or an antigen-binding fragment thereof. 60. The method of any one of claims 51-59, wherein the method further comprises administering a T cell stimulator. 61. The method of claim 60, wherein the T cell stimulator comprises an anti-OX40 antibody or an antigen-binding fragment thereof or an anti-GITR antibody or an antigen-binding fragment thereof. 62. A cell bank comprising one or more recombinant Listeria strains as in any one of claims 1-45. 63. The cell bank of claim 62, wherein the cell bank is a frozen cell bank or a lyophilized cell bank.
